We owe special debt of gratitude to our project supervisor Dr. Nayncy, Head of Department, Department of Computer Science and Engineering ( AI/AIML ), Pranveer Singh Institute of Technology, Kanpur for her constant support and guidance throughout the course of our work. 5. Deployment & Delivery o CI/CD pipelines via GitHub Actions: lint → unit & integration tests → container build → push to registry → automated deployment to staging. o Manual approvals gate production releases; automated rollback on health-check failures. 2.5 Conceptual Diagrams Context Diagram: Shows external actors (patients, hospital admins, providers) interacting with the API Gateway, which fans out to backend services. ● Container Diagram: Illustrates each microservice container, its database, and the message broker, connected by synchronous (REST) and asynchronous (MQ) channels. ● Component Diagram (Search Service): Depicts internal modules: Query Processor → Geospatial Engine → Availability Aggregator → Ranking Engine → Cache Layer. 3.3 Low-Level Design The Low-Level Design (LLD) translates the High-Level architectural vision of MedAppoint into concrete service boundaries, detailed data models, API contracts, and inter-service workflows. While still abstracting away from raw source code, this section dives into the structures and interactions that realize each service’s responsibilities, enabling developers to build against precise specifications. 3.3.1 Service Interfaces and Data Models Each microservice exposes a clearly defined RESTful interface—its API contract—backed by a tailored data model stored in MongoDB. We describe for each key service: 1. Hospital Catalog Service o Primary Entity (“Hospital”): ▪ Attributes: name, address, contact details, location (GeoJSON point), specialties, and an immutable id. 10

▪ Indexes: 2dsphere on location for geospatial operations, compound index on (specialty, active) for filtered retrieval. o API Endpoints (conceptual): ▪ Create Hospital: accepts hospital profile; returns created entity with id and createdAt. ▪ Get Hospital Details: fetches by id, includes computed average rating from embedded user reviews. ▪ Update Hospital: allows patch of mutable fields; returns updated snapshot. ▪ List/Search Hospitals: supports query parameters for bounding-box or radial search, plus specialty filters; returns paginated list enriched with real-time availability summaries. 2. Product & Subscription Service o Primary Entity (“Product”): ▪ Attributes: hospitalId (foreign key), name, category, price, stockQuantity, and optional subscriptionPlan with interval and renewal flags. ▪ Indexes: compound on (hospitalId, category) for fast category-based product listing. o API Endpoints: ▪ Create Product: scoped to a hospital; validates hospitalId matches the caller’s token. ▪ Update/Delete Product: soft-deletes by flagging as inactive to preserve audit, supports incremental stock adjustments. ▪ List Products: filters by hospital, category, and subscription-only flag; returns availability and next-renewal dates for subscriptions. 3. Service Scheduling & Appointment Service o Primary Entity (“ServiceSlot”): 11

event-driven workflows facilitated by a message broker (RabbitMQ). The primary microservices implemented include: ● Hospital Catalog Service ● Product & Subscription Service ● Service Scheduling & Appointment Service ● Cart & Checkout Service ● Search & Ranking Service Each service is implemented with its own database, tailored to its access patterns and consistency needs. We use MongoDB for storing the domain entities, Redis for caching, and RabbitMQ for managing asynchronous events between services. 4.2.1 Hospital Catalog Service The Hospital Catalog Service is responsible for managing hospital profiles. Each hospital has attributes like name, address, contact details, location, and specialties, and it is assigned a unique identifier. The service allows hospital administrators to create, update, and delete hospital records, as well as retrieve detailed information about hospitals. Data Model: ● Hospital Entity: o Attributes: Name, address, contact details, location (GeoJSON point), specialties, and a unique immutable id. o Indexes: ▪ 2dsphere index on location for geospatial queries. ▪ A compound index on specialty and active status for filtered hospital searches. API Endpoints: 1. Create Hospital: Accepts hospital profile data (name, address, specialties, etc.), creates a new hospital record, and returns the created entity with the hospital’s id and creation timestamp. 2. Get Hospital Details: Retrieves hospital details by its unique id, including calculated values such as the average user rating derived from reviews. 3. Update Hospital: Allows updates to mutable fields (e.g., contact details or specialties) and returns the updated hospital
